text: Employees are expected to report to work on time and remain for their full scheduled shift, aside from meal and rest periods. If unable to work a shift, employees must give their supervisor reasonable advance notice, or notify them as soon as practical if the absence is unforeseen.

Failing to show up for work with no notice to a supervisor for more than two consecutive scheduled shifts is treated as voluntary job abandonment/resignation. Absences protected by law, including California paid sick leave, do not count against this policy.

Lactating employees are entitled to reasonable break time and a private lactation space (not a restroom) to express breast milk, with a surface for the pump, a place to sit, electrical access, and nearby access to a sink and a refrigerator or other cooling device. Contact the CEO to request lactation accommodations.
MKA Fitness LLC is the legal business entity that operates Rumble Boxing Sunnyvale.

Employment at Rumble Boxing Sunnyvale (MKA Fitness LLC) is at-will, meaning either the employee or the company may end the employment relationship at any time, with or without reason or advance notice.
Only the CEO of MKA Fitness LLC has the authority to enter into an employment agreement outside the standard at-will terms, and only if it is in writing.

MKA Fitness LLC prohibits harassment, discrimination, and retaliation based on protected categories including race, religion, color, sex/gender, national origin, disability, age (40+), sexual orientation, military/veteran status, and other legally protected characteristics.

Harassment, discrimination, or retaliation complaints can be brought to a supervisor, the designated ombudsperson, the complaint hotline at 571-294-3511, company representative Ashish Chhabra, or Owner Mansi Verma. A written complaint is preferred but not required.
Employees who need a disability or religious accommodation should contact Owner Mansi Verma to discuss the request.
Employees are expected to be polite, courteous, prompt, and attentive to every customer; the general manager should be called immediately if a situation feels difficult to handle.
Never argue with a customer. If a customer remains dissatisfied or a problem develops, ask a supervisor or the general manager to step in.

Being under the influence of, using, possessing, or distributing alcohol, marijuana, or illegal/controlled substances while on the job or on company property is strictly prohibited and can lead to termination.
The company will not discipline employees for marijuana use off-duty and away from the workplace, as long as they are not impaired while on the job.

Employees struggling with alcohol, marijuana, or drug dependency are encouraged to request a treatment or rehabilitation leave; the company will reasonably accommodate treatment, but is not obligated to continue employing someone whose job performance remains impaired.
The first 90 days of continuous employment at MKA Fitness LLC is considered an introductory period, during which a new employee's performance is closely monitored.
Part-time employees are those scheduled to work at least 20 but fewer than 40 hours per week.
Employees on a leave of absence that exceeds any protected state, federal, or local leave period are placed on inactive status.

Bereavement leave provides up to 5 days off (not required to be consecutive) for the death of a family member — defined as a spouse, domestic partner, child, parent, parent-in-law, sibling, grandparent, or grandchild. Employees must have been employed at least 30 days before the leave begins to be eligible, and the leave must be completed within 3 months of the death.

Bereavement leave is unpaid, though employees may use previously accrued paid leave time to cover it. Documentation (e.g. a death certificate, published obituary, or service verification) is required.

The California Family Rights Act (CFRA) provides up to 12 weeks of unpaid family/medical leave in a 12-month period, for employees who have worked at least 12 months (within the last 7 years) and at least 1,250 hours in the prior 12 months.

CFRA leave can be used for the employee's own serious health condition, to care for a family member's serious health condition, for the birth or placement of a child, or for a qualifying military-family exigency. MKA Fitness LLC calculates the 12-month CFRA period using the calendar year.
Employees should contact the CEO as soon as they know they'll need CFRA leave — at least 30 days in advance if the need is foreseeable, such as a planned birth or medical treatment.

category: Employment > Leave > Organ and Bone Marrow Donation
text: Employees who have worked at least 90 days may take up to 30 business days of paid leave (plus up to 30 additional business days of unpaid leave) per year to donate an organ, or up to 5 business days per year to donate bone marrow. The company continues group health plan coverage during this leave.

Employees disabled by pregnancy, childbirth, or a related medical condition may take up to 4 months of pregnancy disability leave (PDL) per pregnancy, as determined by their health care provider; part-time employees receive this on a pro-rated basis.

PDL can be taken intermittently, in increments as small as 15 minutes, and does not need to be continuous. Employees planning PDL should contact the CEO, and must give at least 30 days' notice if the need is foreseeable.

Reproductive loss leave provides up to 5 days off for a qualifying reproductive loss event (failed adoption, failed surrogacy, miscarriage, stillbirth, or an unsuccessful assisted reproduction procedure). Employees must have been employed at least 30 days before the leave begins to be eligible.

Reproductive loss leave is unpaid, though accrued paid leave may be used. Employees are limited to 20 total days of reproductive loss leave in a 12-month period, even if more than one qualifying event occurs.

Nonexempt employees get one paid 10-minute rest break for every 4 hours worked (or major fraction thereof): 1 break for shifts of 3.5-6 hours, 2 breaks for shifts over 6 and up to 10 hours, and 3 breaks for shifts over 10 and up to 14 hours. Rest breaks aren't required for shifts under 3.5 hours.

Nonexempt employees working more than 5 hours in a day get an unpaid, uninterrupted 30-minute meal period, which must start before the end of the 5th hour of work (employees must clock out for it). If the workday is 5-6 hours total, the meal period may be waived by mutual written agreement with a supervisor.

Employees working more than 10 hours in a day get a second unpaid 30-minute meal period, which must start before the end of the 10th hour of work. It may be waivable (with mutual written agreement) if the first meal period was taken and total hours worked don't exceed 12.

For nonexempt employees, hours worked beyond 8 in a workday or 40 in a workweek are paid at 1.5x the regular rate, as are the first 8 hours worked on a 7th consecutive workday. Hours beyond 12 in a workday, or beyond 8 on a 7th consecutive workday, are paid at 2x the regular rate. All overtime must be authorized in advance by a supervisor.
The workday runs from 12:01 a.m. to midnight; the workweek begins each Sunday at 12:01 a.m.
An employee's first performance evaluation takes place after completing one month of employment. Subsequent performance evaluations are conducted every 3 months.
Favorable performance evaluations do not guarantee a raise or promotion — those decisions are solely at the company's discretion.

Employees have the right to inspect or receive a copy of their personnel file; written requests go to the CEO, and the records must be made available within 30 calendar days (up to 35 by mutual written agreement).
Employees have the right to inspect or receive a copy of their payroll records; requests (oral or written) go to the CEO, and records must be provided within 21 calendar days.

Employees who refer a candidate who gets hired can earn a $500 total recruitment bonus, paid in stages: $100 after the new hire completes 1 month of service, $200 after 6 months, and $200 after 1 year. The referral must be documented as the referral source on the applicant's original employment application. If more than one employee referred the same applicant, the bonus is split evenly among them.

All work-related injuries or illnesses must be reported to a supervisor immediately. MKA Fitness LLC maintains an Injury and Illness Prevention Program, available for review in the general manager's office.
MKA Fitness LLC and its insurer will not cover workers' compensation for injuries from voluntary off-duty recreational, social, or athletic activities that aren't part of an employee's job duties.

Company computers, email, electronic communications, and company cell phones are subject to monitoring and review at any time; employees have no expectation of privacy in their use, even for information marked 'private' or password-protected.
The company's CRM system may only be used to reach out to leads/prospects for business purposes — no other outreach is permitted.
Personal cell phones and other personal electronic devices should not be used during work time, except in a genuine emergency; they may be used during breaks and meal periods.

Audio or video recording is prohibited in areas where employees expect privacy, such as restrooms, locker rooms, changing rooms, and lactation rooms, as well as in company meeting rooms and server rooms. Recording private conversations without the consent of all parties is also prohibited.
Personal social media use is prohibited during work time (the company filters social media access on its network), but is allowed during breaks and meal periods on personal devices.
Reference requests for current or former employees must go through the personnel manager; only the dates of employment and last job title are disclosed.
Voluntary resignation occurs if an employee quits or fails to report to work for 3 consecutive scheduled workdays without notifying their supervisor, absent a legally protected reason.

Employees who resign with at least 72 hours' notice receive their final pay on their last day worked; those who resign with less notice receive final pay within 72 hours of resigning. Employees who are involuntarily terminated receive final pay on their last day. All company property must be returned at that time.
MKA Fitness LLC does not permit pay advances against a paycheck or against unaccrued vacation.

Work-related expenses (travel, lodging, meals, required tools/equipment) are reimbursable with supervisor pre-approval; reimbursement requests, with receipts, must be submitted the same day the expense is incurred.

If you believe an improper deduction was made from an exempt employee's salary, report it immediately to your supervisor or the Operations Manager; confirmed improper deductions are promptly reimbursed.
Employees may not accept gifts or gratuities from customers, vendors, or suppliers, as doing so could appear to influence business decisions.
Employees may not conduct personal business or work for another employer during their scheduled working hours at Rumble Boxing Sunnyvale.

Personal or romantic relationships that could impair judgment on the company's behalf (e.g. with a supervisor, subordinate, competitor, or supplier) must be disclosed to a supervisor so the company can assess whether an actual conflict of interest exists.

Confidential company information (financial data, business strategy, supplier/customer details, and anything related to Rumble that shouldn't be shared outside the company) may only be accessed or disclosed on a need-to-know basis, authorized by a supervisor.

MKA Fitness LLC has zero tolerance for workplace violence or threats of violence, including from third parties, even if made in apparent jest. Non-work-related weapons (guns, knives, explosives, or similar) are prohibited at the worksite and company events.
Workplace violence concerns or incidents can be reported to a supervisor, manager, or human resources, or via the workplace violence reporting form.
MKA Fitness LLC provides a confidential employee assistance program (EAP) to help with personal or work-related problems; contact the Operations Manager or CEO/President for more information.
On Mondays, Tuesdays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays, the daily goal is to make 50 outbound calls.
On Fridays, Saturdays, and Sundays, the daily goal is to make 40 outbound calls.
For prospects in the First Time Confirmation category, call them to confirm whether they will attend their upcoming first class.
If a prospect in the First Time Confirmation category does not have a valid phone number, email them directly through LoyalSnap.
Do not remove a prospect from the First Time Confirmation category until they have responded and confirmed they will attend their class.
During class, work in LoyalSnap whenever possible.
During class, regularly check the LoyalSnap inbox and respond to messages as soon as possible.
Member milestones are tracked by rowing distance, including 50,000 meters, 250,000 meters, and 1,000,000 meters, as well as consistent attendance.
Recognizing member milestones makes members feel seen and valued and keeps them motivated to keep coming back.
Community events like member mixers help members build friendships and experience the studio as a community.
Members who build friendships at the studio and experience it as a community stay longer, visit more often, and bring others with them.
A cancellation is more than lost revenue — it's a signal of a mismatch between what the member needs and what the studio is delivering.
Cancellation reasons should be reviewed to find whether the underlying issue is usage, scheduling, coaching, product fit, or satisfaction.
Saving even a small percentage of at-risk members can meaningfully change a studio's monthly performance.
Treat a cancellation request as a chance to listen and solve the member's problem, not just a request to process.
The first 30 days determine whether a new member becomes a long-term client or quietly drifts away.
New member onboarding should make the member feel welcomed and confident, build routine, and create early wins.
New member onboarding includes learning the member's goals, guiding their class selection, and helping them book their next session.
Celebrating a new member's early progress builds personal connection and reinforces the value of their membership.
The Coach Card is used to track a member's goals and progress starting during onboarding.
The gym has two thermostats: one on the left side and one on the right side.
At the end of the day, set both gym thermostats to 85°F or higher to prevent the fans from running overnight.
After each class, wipe down all benches.
To turn off the gym system, tap the red power button in the top-right corner of the trainer stand touchscreen and confirm the shutdown by tapping 'Yes'.
The cleanup crew comes on Wednesday nights and completes most of the cleaning tasks.
At the end of every shift, complete all required closing tasks before locking the studio and leaving.
On days other than Wednesday, vacuum the bathrooms, lobby, studio, and the platform around the trainer stand.
On days other than Wednesday, mop the bathrooms, lobby, studio, and the platform around the trainer stand.
On days other than Wednesday, clean all toilets using the blue toilet cleaner.
On days other than Wednesday, spray Lysol on frequently touched surfaces in the bathrooms.
On days other than Wednesday, spray Lysol on the water fountains.
On days other than Wednesday, spray Lysol on the lockers.
On days other than Wednesday, spray Lysol on the front door handles.
On days other than Wednesday, spray Lysol on the hand sanitizer stations.
On days other than Wednesday, spray Lysol on the gym door handles.
Bring the outdoor A-frame sign and flag inside before closing the studio.
Lock the front doors using the hex key before leaving.
Before leaving, verify that all doors and the lockbox are locked.
Complete all required computer tasks before clocking out.
Respond to any remaining LoyalSnap messages before ending the shift.
Send confirmation messages to all first-time visitors scheduled for the following day before ending the shift.
Tasks assigned to 'Once all members leave' should be completed after the final class of the shift, immediately before closing the studio.
Throughout every shift, regularly check LoyalSnap, Slack, and missed phone calls for new messages that require a response.
If rented gloves were used, spray both the inside and outside of each glove with Lysol.
After disinfecting rented gloves, hang them on the glove drying rack.
Turn on the glove dryer using the small switch on the bottom of the unit.
Leave the glove dryer running for 5 minutes before turning it off.
Never leave the glove dryer running overnight because it is a fire hazard.
Close the gym door after the warm-up begins.
Open the gym door during the cooldown portion of class.
Do not run the washer or dryer between 4:00 PM and 9:00 PM.
Regardless of the day of the week, place all used towels in the dryer at the end of the shift.
Use 1 laundry detergent pod for a small towel load.
Use 2 laundry detergent pods for a large towel load.
When using the washing machine, set the soil level according to the size of the load.
When washing towels at the end of a weekday evening shift, set a 7-hour delay start.
When washing towels at the end of a weekend closing shift, set a 15-hour delay start.
Do not use a delay start when washing towels at the end of the noon shift.
If a member forgets their locker code or cannot open their locker, use the locker key stored in the cabinet to the left of the front desk.
Some lockers remain locked even after using the locker key.
To reset a locker, use the key to place the lock in the unlocked position.
While the locker is unlocked with the key, rotate every number dial downward until it cannot move any farther.
After resetting the number dials, lock the locker, remove the key, and then unlock it normally without the key.
There should always be music playing in the lobby.
Lobby music playlists are available in the Spotify app on the front desk computer.
To turn on the gym system, tap the touchscreen on the trainer stand.
Complete a Slack shift recap before clocking out.
The Slack shift recap should include sales results, member updates, completed operational tasks, cleaning status, and notes for the next shift.
Do not leave the 'Notes for the Next Shift' section blank. Include any member concerns, incomplete tasks, or action items.
A studio succeeds because the small things happen the same way every day, no matter who's on the schedule, not because of one big change.
The Daily Lobby Walkthrough Checklist, GM Time Guide, and General Manager Report Checklist are the core tools for running a consistent shift.
Leads alone don't drive revenue — booked appointments do.
Most studios lose momentum at the booking stage because of uncontacted leads and inconsistent follow-up.
Raising the booking rate also raises show rate and membership conversions automatically, without needing more leads, marketing spend, or discounts.
If a studio is missing its goals, the fastest fix is usually converting existing leads into booked appointments rather than generating more leads.
Audit the ClubReady Bookings Day List regularly to make sure leads are being booked.
Lead types can be pulled in ClubReady to help fill classes that need more attendees.
When a New Lead does not answer the phone, leave a voicemail, record the missed call in LoyalSnap, summarize the voicemail in the notes, and send a follow-up text message.
When a prospect answers the phone, record the conversation in LoyalSnap using the OMG format: Objections, Membership Interest, and Goals.
OMG stands for Objections, Membership Interest, and Goals.
The first-visit-to-membership conversion is the moment revenue becomes real for a new lead.
High-converting studios rely on a repeatable sales process rather than discounts or pressure tactics.
Most conversion problems aren't about price — they're about confidence, clarity, and preparation.
Audit the ClubReady Day List regularly to catch missed sales opportunities.
Filter ClubReady by lead type to build a list of prospects to follow up with.
Use the HOT and Required Response flags in ClubReady Connect to prioritize follow-up with prospects.
Text campaigns, email blasts, and Digital Stack campaigns can all be sent from ClubReady to follow up with prospects before they walk in.
A steady flow of new leads is the foundation of studio revenue — bookings, shows, new member sales, referrals, and revenue all depend on it.
The studio should generate leads proactively every week rather than waiting for leads to come in on their own.
Low lead flow pushes the team into reactive mode: heavy discounting, cold outreach, and end-of-month panic.
Healthy lead flow makes sales, staffing, and retention easier across the board.
Certified Agency Partners (CAP) are the studio's approved digital marketing vendors for lead generation.
Franchise Focused Marketing, ClubBuzz, and Franchise Ramp are Certified Agency Partners for digital lead generation.
A discounted health & wellness business membership can be used as a low-cost email lead generator, run monthly or quarterly by the Owner, General Manager, or Studio Lead.
Front desk staff should prospect on the ground with an editable prospecting card during down time on shift.
Fishbowls or lead boxes placed in the studio are a low-cost, passive lead generation tool run by front desk staff during down time.
Direct mailers, typically printed at 11 inches by 6 inches, are a lead generation channel the studio can use.
Grassroots lead generation includes B2B partnerships, community events, and corporate partnerships.
Referral types, landing pages, and QR codes can be built in ClubReady to support grassroots lead generation campaigns.
To turn an existing web link, like a ClubReady direct-purchase package or the studio's website, into a QR code, use a QR generator site such as qrcode-monkey.com.
Memberships automatically renew every month until they are cancelled.
When purchasing a new membership, members may choose to pay a $99 enrollment fee to receive a new pair of gloves and a new pair of hand wraps.
If a new member does not want gloves and hand wraps, the enrollment fee is waived to $0.
When handling objections, first understand the member's concerns before offering a solution.
If a member says they are in a rush, acknowledge their time and explain that the conversation will only take about a minute.
If a member says they need time to think, ask what they are specifically unsure about before responding.
If a member does not like recurring monthly payments, recommend class packages instead of memberships.
If a member is concerned about package expiration dates, consider extending the expiration date when appropriate.
Match your recommendation to the member's specific concerns whenever possible.
The goal of a sales conversation is to help the member find the option that best fits their needs, not simply to make a sale.
When handling objections, first understand the member's concern before trying to solve it.
Do not immediately argue against an objection. Ask questions to understand the member's concerns first.
If a member says they are in a rush, remind them that they have already invested time in attending class and learning about memberships is the next step toward reaching their fitness goals.
Understanding the real concern allows you to recommend a better solution.
If a member does not like recurring monthly payments, recommend a class package instead of a membership.
If a member is concerned about class package expiration dates, consider extending the expiration date when appropriate.
Most sales objections have practical solutions that can be matched to the member's specific concern.
Tailor your recommendation to the individual member instead of giving every member the same recommendation.
Address objections honestly and confidently.
The goal of a sales conversation is to help the member find the option that best fits their needs rather than simply making a sale.
Class packages are one-time purchases and do not automatically renew.
The regular price of the 5-class package is $189.
The regular price of the 10-class package is $339.
The regular price of the 20-class package is $639.
Membership cancellations require a 30-day notice before the cancellation takes effect.
Members must email sunnyvale@rumbleboxinggym.com to request cancellation of their membership.
Members may cancel a class reservation without penalty if they cancel at least 12 hours before the scheduled class.
Cancelling a class reservation less than 12 hours before the scheduled class results in a $15 late cancellation fee.
A single drop-in class costs $48.
There are several membership options: 4 classes per month, 6 classes per month, 8 classes per month, 12 classes per month, and Unlimited.
The regular price of the 4 classes per month membership is $109 per month.
The regular price of the 6 classes per month membership is $119 per month.
The regular price of the 8 classes per month membership is $169 per month.
The regular price of the 12 classes per month membership is $179 per month.
The regular price of the Unlimited membership is $209 per month.
Before members arrive, identify potential sales candidates using PIQ.
Members who have run out of class credits should be treated as high-priority sales candidates.
Members with low remaining credits should be treated as medium-priority sales candidates.
Members with limited memberships who consistently use all of their credits before renewal are good candidates for membership upgrades.
Use ClubReady to review a member's attendance history when determining whether they are a good upgrade candidate.
When members arrive, identify unfamiliar members by checking their assigned spot in PIQ so you know who to speak with after class.
During class, review each sales candidate's bookings, remaining credits, invoices, and account details before speaking with them.
Low remaining credits do not always mean a member needs to renew because future class bookings also use available credits.
Before speaking with a prospect, review their LoyalSnap notes, previous conversations, phone calls, tours, and client notes.
Prioritize sales prospects based on both how likely they are to purchase and the potential value of the purchase.
Whenever possible, assign a sales conversation to the team member who has the strongest existing relationship with the member.
After class, begin conversations with the highest-priority prospect who is still in the studio before moving on to lower-priority prospects.
Begin sales conversations naturally by asking the member what their favorite part of class was.
Ask members how many times per week they expect to attend so you can recommend the most appropriate membership.
Recommend one or two membership options that best fit the member's goals and expected attendance.
Use the plans sheet as a visual aid when explaining membership options.
After presenting membership options, allow the member time to think instead of continuing to talk.
Prepare a sales game plan before members arrive.
Members who have no remaining class credits are high-priority sales candidates because they need additional classes to continue attending.
Members with low remaining class credits are medium-priority sales candidates.
Members with limited memberships who consistently use all of their credits before renewal are good candidates for upgrading their membership.
Use ClubReady to review a member's attendance history when deciding whether they are a good upgrade candidate.
Before class begins, learn to recognize each sales candidate by checking their assigned spot in PIQ.
If you do not recognize a member, use their assigned spot in PIQ to identify them in the studio.
The purpose of identifying members before class is to make it easier to speak with them after class.
During class, review each sales candidate's account before speaking with them.
Review bookings, remaining credits, invoices, attendance history, and other relevant account information before beginning a sales conversation.
Low available credits do not always mean a member needs to purchase more classes because future bookings also use available credits.
Review LoyalSnap notes, previous phone calls, tours, conversations, and client notes before speaking with a sales prospect.
The more you know about a prospect before the conversation, the better you can recommend the right membership or package.
Prioritize sales prospects based on both their likelihood of purchasing and the potential value of the purchase.
Speak with the highest-priority prospects before speaking with lower-priority prospects.
If multiple employees are working, divide sales prospects evenly among the team.
Whenever possible, the employee with the strongest existing relationship with a member should conduct the sales conversation.
A strong relationship may come from previous phone calls, tours, or earlier sales conversations.
After class, work through your prioritized prospect list one member at a time.
If a prospect leaves before you can speak with them, continue to the next highest-priority prospect who is still in the studio.
Begin sales conversations naturally instead of immediately discussing memberships.
A good opening question is: 'What was your favorite part of class?'
Ask members how many times per week they expect to attend classes.
Use a member's fitness goals and expected attendance to recommend the most appropriate membership.
Recommend one or two membership options that best fit the member's needs instead of presenting every available option.
Use the plans sheet as a visual aid while explaining memberships and packages.
After presenting your recommendation, pause and allow the member time to think.
Avoid filling silence after presenting membership options because members often need time to consider the recommendation.
During the July 2026 promotion, the 8 classes per month membership costs $152 per month when purchased in person.
During the July 2026 promotion, the Unlimited membership costs $157 per month when purchased in person.
During the July 2026 promotion, customers who purchase the 10-class package in person receive 14 total classes for $339.
The July 2026 promotion adds 4 bonus classes to the 10-class package at no additional cost.
Quick win revenue is money already inside the studio's system that just hasn't been claimed yet.
Quick win revenue sources include expired packs, inactive members, past dues, low-usage members, missed follow-ups, and unclosed leads.
Quick win revenue converts fast, takes little effort, and carries no extra cost since it's 100% margin.
The Re-Sign Soon report identifies members whose membership is about to expire so they can be re-signed quickly.
The 4x-per-month membership upsell targets members who are using their membership often enough to justify a higher tier.
A renewal rate audit and the Declined Charges report, filtered by card on file, are used to recover quick win revenue from lapsed or failed payments.
Referrals are the highest-converting, lowest-cost lead source available to a studio.
Referred members convert faster, stay longer, and need less selling because trust already exists before they walk in.
The 4 referral pillars are Always Ask, Referral Contests, Referral Programs (Evergreen), and Mobile App Referral.
Always ask every member for a referral rather than waiting for referrals to happen on their own.
Members with expired packs or missed sales can still be asked for referrals even if they haven't purchased.
Email and text campaigns can be sent to prospects specifically to ask for referrals.
The Monthly Sales Planner breaks the studio's monthly revenue target into daily, role-based tasks focused on leads, bookings, show rate, and conversions.
Daily revenue gap tracking lets the team catch and close performance gaps throughout the month instead of only discovering shortfalls at month-end.
The General Manager completes the Monthly Sales Planner; the Owner reviews it.
The Monthly Sales Planner is filled out on the first business day of the month, then updated daily.
A booked appointment isn't revenue until the person actually shows up — no-shows are the biggest revenue leak in a studio.
Improving show rate grows revenue without needing more leads, advertising, or discounts.
Confirmation messages before a first visit should communicate confidence, relevance, urgency, and personalization.
ClubReady's First Visits: Past Bookings report flags missed sales opportunities from people who didn't show.
ClubReady's First Visits: Future Bookings report audits the pipeline of upcoming first-time visitors so they can be confirmed.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > BOGO Next Class Booking > Context
text: Use this voicemail when following up with a customer who attended a BOGO promotional class and needs encouragement to schedule their next class.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > BOGO Next Class Booking > Guidelines
text: Mention that the customer did well in their previous session and offer assistance booking their next class. Encourage a simple call or text response.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > BOGO Next Class Booking > Purpose
text: The purpose of this voicemail is to reconnect after the customer's first session, acknowledge their experience, and help them continue attending classes.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > BOGO Next Class Booking > Script
text: Hey {member_name}, this is {employee_name} from Rumble Boxing Sunnyvale. Hope you're doing well! I wanted to reach out and help you get your next class booked. You did great in your last session, and we'd love to have you back in the studio. Give me a call or shoot me a text when you get a chance, and we'll get you set up.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > First Class Confirmation > Context
text: Use this voicemail when a customer has booked their first class at Rumble Boxing Sunnyvale and needs confirmation before attending.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > First Class Confirmation > Guidelines
text: Tell the customer to arrive approximately 25 minutes early for check-in and a pre-class introduction. Explain that gloves and wraps can be brought if they have them, but rentals and wraps are available at the studio. Offer assistance with any questions before class.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > First Class Confirmation > Purpose
text: The purpose of this voicemail is to confirm the customer's first class, reduce uncertainty before their first visit, explain arrival expectations, and make sure they know what equipment is available.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > First Class Confirmation > Restrictions
text: Do not tell customers they must own boxing equipment before attending. Do not assume they already know the studio process or class expectations.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > First Class Confirmation > Script
text: Hey {member_name}, this is {employee_name} from Rumble Boxing Sunnyvale. Just confirming you’re all set for your first class tomorrow. Please arrive about 25 minutes early so we can get you checked in and go through a quick pre-class intro. If you have gloves and wraps, feel free to bring them — otherwise we do have rentals and wraps available at the studio. If you have any questions before class, give us a call or text.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > First Timer After Free Credit Class > Context
text: Use this voicemail when following up with a first-time guest after they have completed their free credit class.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > First Timer After Free Credit Class > Guidelines
text: Thank the customer for attending, ask how their experience went, learn what they want from a workout program, and explain that membership options and promotions may be available depending on their goals.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > First Timer After Free Credit Class > Purpose
text: The purpose of this voicemail is to collect feedback, understand the customer's fitness goals, and introduce appropriate membership or promotion options based on their needs.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > First Timer After Free Credit Class > Restrictions
text: Do not immediately assume the customer wants a membership. First understand their experience and goals before recommending options.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > First Timer After Free Credit Class > Script
text: Hey {member_name}, this is {employee_name} from Rumble Boxing Sunnyvale. I just wanted to thank you again for coming in and taking your first class with us. I was hoping to get your feedback on how everything went, learn a little more about your fitness goals, and see what you're looking for in a workout program. We also have a few membership options and promotions available that may be a great fit depending on your goals. Give me a call or shoot me a text when you get a chance, and I'd be happy to help.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > General Rules > Context
text: Voicemail scripts should be selected based on the customer's current stage in the Rumble Boxing Sunnyvale customer journey. Different scripts apply to leads, first-time guests, BOGO customers, recurring members, milestones, and birthdays.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > General Rules > Guidelines
text: Always personalize voicemail scripts by replacing placeholders with the customer's name and employee's name. Match the voicemail to the customer's actual situation before sending it. Use LoyalSnap notes, previous conversations, attendance history, bookings, and account information when available to understand the customer context.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > General Rules > Purpose
text: The purpose of voicemail scripts is to create a personal connection, provide helpful follow-up, encourage engagement, and guide customers toward their next appropriate action. The goal is to support the customer experience rather than simply make a sale.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > General Rules > Restrictions
text: Do not assume a customer has attended a class, purchased a membership, or understands Rumble Boxing offerings unless that information is confirmed. Do not use a script intended for one customer stage with a different customer stage.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > Member Birthday > Context
text: Use this voicemail when contacting a member on their birthday.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > Member Birthday > Guidelines
text: Wish the member a happy birthday, thank them for being part of the Rumble family, and let them know the studio looks forward to seeing them again.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > Member Birthday > Purpose
text: The purpose of this voicemail is to celebrate the member, strengthen the relationship with the Rumble Boxing Sunnyvale community, and show appreciation.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > Member Birthday > Script
text: Hey {member_name}, this is {employee_name} from Rumble Boxing Sunnyvale. Just wanted to wish you a happy birthday and hope you're having an awesome day! Thanks for being part of our Rumble family. We look forward to seeing you back in the studio soon. Enjoy your special day!

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > Member Milestone > Context
text: Use this voicemail when a member reaches an important class attendance milestone.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > Member Milestone > Guidelines
text: Congratulate the member on reaching their class milestone, thank them for their effort and consistency, and encourage them to continue attending.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > Member Milestone > Purpose
text: The purpose of this voicemail is to recognize the member's achievement, strengthen the relationship with the studio, and celebrate their fitness journey.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > Member Milestone > Script
text: Hey {member_name}, this is {employee_name} from Rumble Boxing Sunnyvale. I wanted to personally reach out and congratulate you on hitting your {milestone} class! That's a huge accomplishment, and we're proud to be part of your fitness journey. Thank you for showing up, putting in the work, and bringing great energy to the studio. We can't wait to see you back for the next one!

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > Recurring Member Next Class Booking > Context
text: Use this voicemail when contacting a recurring member who has not attended Rumble Boxing Sunnyvale recently.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > Recurring Member Next Class Booking > Guidelines
text: Acknowledge that the member has not been seen recently, ask how they are doing, and encourage them to return to the studio. Offer to help schedule their next class.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > Recurring Member Next Class Booking > Purpose
text: The purpose of this voicemail is to reconnect with an existing member, encourage them to return, and support their ongoing fitness goals.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > Recurring Member Next Class Booking > Restrictions
text: Do not treat recurring members like new leads. They already have a relationship with the studio and should receive a retention-focused message.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > Recurring Member Next Class Booking > Script
text: Hey {member_name}, this is {employee_name} from Rumble Boxing Sunnyvale. Hope you're doing well! We haven't seen you in the studio in a little while, so I wanted to personally reach out and see how everything's going. We'd love to get you back on the bags and help you stay on track with your fitness goals. Give me a call or shoot me a text when you get a chance, and let's get your next class booked. Looking forward to seeing you back in the studio soon!

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > Unscheduled Lead > Context
text: Use this voicemail when contacting an unscheduled lead who has shown interest in Rumble Boxing Sunnyvale but has not yet booked a class. The lead may have submitted interest information, visited the website, or otherwise interacted with Rumble without starting their first class.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > Unscheduled Lead > Guidelines
text: When following up with an unscheduled lead, ask about their goals, what they are looking for in a workout program, and why they considered Rumble. Encourage them to call or text back so the team can help them get started.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > Unscheduled Lead > Purpose
text: The purpose of this voicemail is to create a personal connection, understand the lead's fitness goals, learn what they are looking for in a fitness program, and discover what sparked their interest in Rumble Boxing.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > Unscheduled Lead > Restrictions
text: Do not assume the lead has attended a class, purchased a package, or is ready to buy a membership. The first goal is understanding their needs and starting a conversation.

category: Sales > Voicemail Scripts > Unscheduled Lead > Script
text: Hey {member_name}, this is {employee_name} from Rumble Boxing Sunnyvale. I saw you checked us out and wanted to reach out personally. I'd love to learn more about what you're looking for in a fitness program, what your goals are, and what sparked your interest in Rumble. Give me a call or shoot me a text when you get a chance, and we'll find the best way to get you started.
The Morning Shift runs Monday through Friday, from 5:30 AM until 7:30 AM.
The Noon Shift runs Monday through Friday, from 11:00 AM until 1:00 PM.
On Mondays and Wednesdays, the Evening Shift runs from 3:45 PM until 8:15 PM.
On Tuesdays and Thursdays, the Evening Shift runs from 5:00 PM until 8:15 PM.
On Fridays, the Evening Shift runs from 5:00 PM until 7:15 PM.
On Saturdays, the All Day Shift runs from 7:45 AM until 12:45 PM.
On Sundays, the All Day Shift runs from 7:45 AM until 12:45 PM.
Use ClubReady to review a member's attendance history.
ClubReady helps determine whether a member regularly uses all of their available class credits.
Members who consistently use all of their credits before renewal may be good candidates for a membership upgrade.
ClubReady's Leads Added report tracks how many new leads the studio is generating.
ClubReady's Bookings Day List shows scheduled appointments so staff can audit them and follow up.
ClubReady's New Members report shows every member who joined.
ClubReady's Agreement Log shows new members, packs, upsells, and downsells.
ClubReady's Gross Sales report and Invoices Coming Due report are used for daily revenue tracking.
ClubReady's Lost Members report and Cancellation Reasons report are used to track and analyze attrition.
ClubReady's Declined Charges report can be filtered by card on file to find failed payments to recover.
ClubReady Connect can flag prospects as HOT or Required Response to prioritize follow-up.
Referral types, landing pages, and QR codes can all be created and managed inside ClubReady.
Review LoyalSnap notes before speaking with a sales prospect.
LoyalSnap stores notes from previous conversations with members.
LoyalSnap stores records of previous phone calls.
LoyalSnap stores notes from member tours.
LoyalSnap stores client notes that help personalize future sales conversations.
Reviewing LoyalSnap history before speaking with a member helps you recommend the most appropriate membership.
Use PIQ before members arrive to identify potential sales candidates.
Members with no remaining class credits can be identified in PIQ.
Members with low remaining class credits can be identified in PIQ.
Use PIQ to determine each member's assigned spot in the studio.
A member's assigned spot in PIQ can be used to identify them before class.
PIQ is used before class to prepare for sales conversations.
Prepare a sales game plan before members arrive.
The recommended sales game plan is maintained in a Google Sheet.
Create a new dated game plan for each shift.
Record sales candidates and their priority level in the sales game plan.
Prioritize members as High, Medium, or Low priority in the sales game plan.
The sales game plan helps organize which members should be spoken to first after class.
Check PIQ before every shift for first-time visitors and member milestones.
Record the first name and assigned spot of every first-time visitor.
Record the first name and class count of every member celebrating a milestone.
If there are no first-time visitors or milestones for a shift, record that there are none.
Follow the Milestone SOP whenever a member reaches a milestone.
Welcome every first-time visitor by giving them a tour of the studio before class.
Help first-time visitors purchase gloves and hand wraps before their first class if needed.
ClubReady is used to review member accounts, attendance history, memberships, class packages, invoices, and billing information.
LoyalSnap is the customer relationship management (CRM) system used to track member communication, notes, calls, emails, and sales history.
PIQ is used to manage class attendance, member assignments, and identify sales opportunities before class.
